author: @MichaelSkinner-e9j
text:
Places like India, Africa, China, and other parts of the world need this.  Fresh potable water in those places is a Huge Problem, and they need to not be exploited or scalped.

Graphene water desalinization and filtration will be necessary not just in the coming years, but even now.

California experienced this when their snow pack was less, and that was “Civil”- there are other areas that aren’t so orderly when water is scarce and you are competing with industry, farming, and even residential.

author: @luvbotany
text:
Love your videos as it shows people that science and 'invention' isnt hard, just take hands on and thinking out problems. I was working on a project a few years ago that included much of what your talking about. My guess is that you have some uncarbonized humic type compounds in your material. Activated carbon is fully carbonized so there isnt alot of loose functional groups and long chain carbons which is where the 'hardness' number comes in. This is why you have good number but the water really isnt clean because humic compounds are taking up and leaching slowly out, which isnt good. And the numbers will go down quickly if you have not fully opened up the carbon matrix. There are solutions to all these problems which is a much more engineered process and refinement. I think the path you're on is exciting but i would have concerns when it comes to water consumption until alot of hoops are jumped thru.

Carbon is a great compound to use and Im looking forward to its advancement in all sorts of fields, like water filter.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i know what you mean about hoops mate

author: @RajendraJagad
text: Thanks Robert, Nice video, Distilled water has a conductivity in the range of 0.5 to 3 µmhos/cm and since you were holding the probes about cm apart, 400ohms is consider high.But then again distill water is not consider healthy compaired to mineral waters for drinking purposes. Since temperature contributes to conductivity it might help if we know the temperature. But very impressive video thanks.
